exemplar.title: To what extent are elements of Romanticism used to portray Tess' character and her downfall in "Tess of the D'Urbervilles" by Thomas Hardy?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent are elements of Romanticism used to portray Tess' character and her downfall in "Tess of the D'Urbervilles" by Thomas Hardy?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Clear, accurate and effective communication of topic with strong signposting and sustained focus on the research question.
Methodology is comprehensive, with a well-justified selection of close reading and secondary sources.
Research question is explicitly stated and tightly integrated into the structure at multiple points.
weaknesses: Occasional dense theoretical asides and minor digressions slightly obscure concision and razor-sharp focus.
Transitions into some counter-arguments (e.g. Martell) feel abrupt and could be more smoothly integrated.
Could more explicitly restate at each analytical turn how the evidence directly answers the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand commentary placed the work solidly in the 5â6 band, with only minor lapses preventing exemplary precision. Deducting one mark reflects those small moments of digression and density while recognizing the overall clarity and methodological coherence.

strengths: Demonstrates substantial and coherent knowledge of Hardyâs text and critical discourse, underpinned by 30+ scholarly sources.
Terminology (Romanticism, Realism, binary opposition, Death Drive, chronotope) is deployed accurately and consistently.
Primary quotations and secondary insights are generally well integrated, supporting the studentâs independent line of thought.
weaknesses: Over-complex syntax and dense clusters of citation occasionally impede the seamless flow of argument.
A few terms (e.g. âanti-Romanticistâ) are coined rather awkwardly, detracting slightly from terminological precision.
A small number of source references are summarised rather than fully synthesized, limiting the seamless application expected at the highest level.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work clearly sits at the top of the 3â4 band approaching the 5â6 band, with only minor inconsistencies preventing a flawless 6. A one-mark deduction acknowledges these small lapses while crediting the essayâs deep and effective use of literary knowledge and sources.

strengths: Research is wide-ranging, current, and consistently aligned with the research question, drawing on primary text and diverse critical voices.
Analysis is deep and nuanced, linking textual detail to broader theoretical frameworks with well-supported conclusions.
Argumentation is focused and evaluative, engaging critically with counter-readings and synthesizing multiple perspectives into a coherent overall conclusion.
weaknesses: A few passages lean toward accumulation of viewpoints rather than fully interrogating each criticâs assumptions.
Some assertions (e.g. Marxismâs influence) could be more tightly supported with explicit textual evidence.
The concluding reflection on broader implications could be more fully developed to heighten the evaluative impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a handful of moments that could have deployed more original interrogation, the studentâs consistent relevance of research, depth of analysis, and critical evaluation place the work at the top of the 10â12 band. Awarding the full 12 acknowledges sustained excellence across all three strands.

strengths: Structure and layout fully satisfy IB conventions, with clear headings, contents page, page numbers, and complete bibliography.
Formatting of block quotations and in-text citations is consistently correct and professional.
Overall presentation is polished and enables easy navigation without detracting issues.
weaknesses: Occasional over-long paragraphs slightly affect readability but do not undermine the overall professionalism.
Minor typographical spacing inconsistencies appear but are infrequent and non-distracting.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay meets all criteria for the highest band in presentation. Minor typographical and paragraph-length issues are too trivial to warrant any deduction.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The methodology is clearly articulated: close textual analysis supported by a selection of literary-critical, philosophical, and historical sources.  
â¢ The student justifies source choice (e.g., LÃ©vi-Strauss for binary opposition, Freud for Death Drive) and shows awareness of theoretical frames, evidencing informed selection.  
â¢ While the range of secondary scholarship is wide and mostly relevant, the essay could have explained data-gathering procedures (e.g., criteria for source inclusion) more explicitly; nevertheless, the approach is complete and fit for purpose, meriting a high band score.

The layout adheres to IB requirements: title, word count (3 853), structured contents page, consistent in-text citations, complete bibliography, and (as indicated) page numbering.  
â¢ Formatting of headings and block quotations is clear, enabling easy navigation and comprehension.  
â¢ There are very occasional minor typographical mis-spacings and some unconventional bold/italic choices, but these do not detract from the overall professionalism; hence the strand fully satisfies the highest band.

The bibliography is extensive, drawing on journal articles, monographs, and primary text, with clear academic provenance.  
â¢ Most sources directly inform the argument (e.g., Alden on industrial change, Christoff on solitude), showing a **strong alignment with the research question**.  
â¢ A small proportion of sources are referenced only briefly or in passing, suggesting scope for tighter selectivity, yet the overall research base is wide, current, and substantively relevant, justifying placement in the 7â9 band at an upper-middle level.

The studentâs introduction delineates the literary territoryâVictorian tensions between Realism and Romanticismâand immediately specifies Hardyâs Tess as the vehicle for exploration.  
â¢ Clear signposting (title, reiterated research question, overview of focus on setting and solitude) gives readers an accurate sense of purpose and scope.  
â¢ Minor lapses in concision (occasional digressions and dense phrasing) slightly blur the line of argument, but overall the topic remains coherently and engagingly communicated throughout the essay.

The argument follows a logical two-part structure (setting; solitude) culminating in a synthesising conclusion, and engages with counter-readings (e.g., Christoff vs. Martell) to show **some critical evaluation**.  
â¢ The student acknowledges limitations in theories (e.g., LÃ©vi-Straussâ neglect of materialism), indicating evaluative thought.  
â¢ However, the discussion occasionally leans toward accumulation rather than interrogation of sources, and the implications for the research question could be drawn out more sharply; thus the work fits securely in the 7-9 band but does not reach the highest analytical finesse.

The essay offers **coherent, well-developed analyses** of setting, imagery, and thematic tension, moving confidently between primary evidence and critical views.  
â¢ Quotations are unpacked with attention to diction, syntax, and symbolism, and linked to broader historical and theoretical frames.  
â¢ Nonetheless, some analytical passages become descriptive or digressive (e.g., extended paraphrase of critics) and a few assertions (e.g., on Marxismâs influence) are not fully substantiated, limiting the sustained sharpness needed for higher than a 7.

The essay follows the IB EE conventions: title page with required details, clear abstract-like introduction, logical section headings, table of contents, conclusion, and complete bibliography.  
â¢ Transitions between major sections are smooth, and sub-headings mirror the articulated methodology, demonstrating a well-considered structure that supports the progression of the argument.  
â¢ Minor issues (occasional over-long paragraphs) do not impede overall clarity, so the work meets all expectations for the top band.

The essay demonstrates **substantial literary knowledge**: detailed narrative of Hardyâs settings, nuanced grasp of Romantic and Realist conventions, and confident integration of 30+ scholarly sources.  
â¢ Quotations from primary and secondary texts are aptly selected and woven into the studentâs own prose, showing an independent, coherent understanding of the novel and its critical conversation.  
â¢ A handful of source references remain briefly summarised rather than fully synthesised, which limits the seamlessness expected for a perfect 6, but overall knowledge and use of sources are clearly excellent.

Subject-specific terminologyâRomanticism, Realism, pastoral, pathetic fallacy, binary opposition, Thanatosâis employed **accurately and regularly**.  
â¢ Concepts from literary theory (Marxist criticism, Freudian psychoanalysis) are introduced with correct definitions and applied to textual moments.  
â¢ Occasional over-complex syntax and dense citation clusters hinder clarity, and a few terms (e.g., âanti-Romanticistâ) are inelegantly coined, preventing flawless consistency, but precision and relevance remain high.

The research question is explicitly stated in the title, in bold within the introduction, and reiterated at key transition points, ensuring the reader never loses sight of the guiding inquiry.  
â¢ Most sections, sub-headings, and paragraph topics map directly onto the wording of the question (âto what extentâ, âelements of Romanticismâ, âdownfallâ), demonstrating a strong, sustained focus.  
â¢ A few analytical detours (e.g., lengthy theoretical asides on LÃ©vi-Strauss or Marx) momentarily loosen the tightness of focus, preventing the exemplary precision required for a 6 but solidly fulfilling the 5â6 band.

annotations.comment: This section relies heavily on lengthy quotations; summarizing some passages would restore your analytical voice and maintain argument momentum.
Your close reading of star imagery in the beehive scene demonstrates exceptional command of figurative language analysis.
Bonicaâs theoretical perspective is applied but not interrogated; critically evaluate its relevance to enhance the discussionâs depth.
The binary opposition framework is applied cogently here, with a clear linkage between LÃ©vi-Straussâs theory and textual evidence.
This paragraph would benefit from a concluding sentence that explicitly ties the analysis back to the research question for clearer coherence.
The comparative reading of Siebenschuhâs concept of loneliness adds depth by situating solitude within a broader Romantic discourse.
This section asserts strong claims but needs specific textual examples illustrating the psychological impact of solitude on Tess.
The title and research question are clearly stated at the outset, fulfilling IB formatting requirements and establishing a precise focus for the investigation.
The research question is concise, focused, and effectively anchors the investigation throughout the essay.
The student provides an accurate word count within the document, satisfying the IB requirement for formatting.
The table of contents is wellâorganized with clear section titles and page references, enhancing the essayâs navigability.
The introduction provides extensive context but could be more concise; consider reducing dense theoretical asides to maintain a sharper focus on the research question.
Reiterating the research question at the start of the section reinforces coherence and keeps the reader aligned with the investigationâs focus.
Defining Romanticism and realism with sourced definitions establishes a strong theoretical framework and demonstrates accurate use of subjectâspecific concepts.
Paragraph transitions into the methodology section could be signposted more clearly; adding an overview of source selection criteria would enhance structural clarity.
The close reading of Hardyâs Wessex setting exemplifies strong analytical depth, linking textual detail convincingly to the Romantic-Realist tension.
This paragraph covers multiple ideas in one block; breaking it into smaller units would improve readability and sharpen the emphasis of each analytical point.
Insightful use of personification and pathetic fallacy reflects accurate application of literary terminology and deep understanding of Romantic devices.
The statement that Hardy âflatly rejects Romanticismâ is too absolutist; consider acknowledging subtler Romantic elements to add nuance to your argument.
The synesthetic analysis of Tessâs sensory experiences demonstrates excellent depth and precise use of literary concepts.
Effectively engages with multiple critics to construct a nuanced argument about the gardenâs symbolism, showing high-level critical evaluation.
The integration of Martellâs critique feels abrupt; improve flow by previewing this counter-argument earlier or providing a clearer transition.
Your use of Treziseâs chronotope concept to interpret Stonehenge is insightful and demonstrates sophisticated theoretical engagement.
Analysis relies on extended quoted interpretation; expanding original commentary will deepen your independent analytical voice.
The introduction of solitude as a thematic lens is effectively signposted and maintains clear alignment with your research question.
Consider explicitly restating how each analysis of solitude contributes to answering the research question to preserve a consistent focus.
Integrating Freudâs Death Drive theory showcases strong interdisciplinary insight and adds valuable psychological depth to Tessâs characterization.
The discussion of Tessâs dialogue and hyperbole effectively links linguistic choices to her psychological state, showing strong analytical insight.
This paragraph contains an overly complex sentence that impairs clarity; consider splitting it into shorter, more focused sentences.
While Freudâs psychoanalysis is introduced, more explicit linkage to specific text passages would reinforce the theoretical application.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes the key themes of setting and solitude, demonstrating a coherent methodological closure.
The conclusion reiterates earlier arguments but could be strengthened with reflective commentary on broader implications or real-world connections.
The bibliography is comprehensive, demonstrating extensive research and effective use of a variety of scholarly sources.
URL formatting and access date placement vary across entries; standardize citation style (e.g., MLA or Chicago) throughout the bibliography.
Bibliographic entries exhibit inconsistent indentation and line breaks; apply a uniform hanging-indent style for professional presentation.
